title: A surveyor's benchmark datum mark: a gold equilateral triangle inscribed on a dark stone tablet, with a small circle at the centroid — the exact fixed point. Precept's compiled definition is the datum: an immovable reference from which all runtime behavior is calculated. If the datum moves, all measurements become invalid; if the definition holds, every outcome is predictable.
conceptualBridge: The survey datum means Precept's definition is the immovable reference because every runtime measurement is derived from the fixed coordinates the contract declares.
decision: Shortlisted after iteration: avg 4.38, no hard fails — v2 simplified to bold triangle outline + datum circle; the gold-on-dark survey benchmark is the only candidate from this visual tradition; strong uniqueness and clear legibility at 32px.
